content.full_text:
STATE OF CALIFORNIA
EDMUND G. BROWN JR., Governor

FAIR POLITICAL PRACTICES COMMISSION
M Street, Suite 522
no 95814
916) 322 5660

July 29, 1975

Mr. Paul B. Pressman
City Attorney
City of Vista
Post Office Box 188
Vista, California 92083

Dear Mr. Pressman:

Thank you for your letter of July 8, 1975, requesting the
Fair Political Practices Commission to issue an opinion on two
factual situations dealing with potential conflicts of interest.

Your request for a formal opinion is denied because the
Commission will not accept opinion requests from anyone other
than the person whose duties are affected under the Act. Although
an opinion request may be submitted through counsel or other representatives, the identity of the person requesting the opinion
must be disclosed.

For your information, the member of the Parks and Recreation
Commission and the Chairman of the Beautification Committee would
be considered public officials under Section 82048 of the Government Code which states that the term public official means "every
member, officer, employee or consultant of a state or local
government agency." However, both of these persons may appear
on behalf of their clients before the City Council and other
agencies in their private capacities. On the other hand, while
serving on their respective commissions, neither may make a
decision which could have a material financial effect on any
source of income of $250 or more.

Although for the reason stated no formal opinion will be
issued, I hope the foregoing is sufficiently helpful. If you
have any questions, I urge you to contact a member of the
Commission staff.

Sincerely,

Daniel H. Lowenstein
Chairman, for the
Commission

DHL:cjb

P.O. BOX 182 • 200 W. BROADWAY • VIETA, CALIFORNIA 92683

July 8, 1975.


Fair Political Practices Commission,
926 "J" Street,
Suite 522,
Sacramento, California 95814.

Re: Request for Opinion
    - Conflict of Interest
      Members of City Commissions, Committees
      and Boards

Dear Commissioners:

It is respectfully requested that the Commission issue an
opinion pursuant to Section 33114 of the Government Code with
respect to the two factual situations set forth below answering
the three questions set forth below. When such questions arise,
it becomes our duty, as City Attorney, to advise the individuals
in question as well as the City officer or employee concerned,
whether or not the individual has a conflict of interest which
would disqualify him or her from attempting to influence a
governmental decision.

FACTUAL SITUATION NO. 1:

A member of the Parks and Recreation Commission of the City
is an attorney representing a client who owns real property which
is the subject of a rezoning proposal before the Planning Commission and the City Council. The client has paid or promised the
attorney $250 or more in fees within 12 months prior to his representation of the client before the Planning Commission and City
Council.

The Parks and Recreation Commission is an advisory commission of the City created by ordinance and composed of seven
appointees of the City Council. The members are not paid for
their services. Its powers and duties are set forth in the
ordinance as follows:

                                          Cont'd....

Fair Political Practices Commission
July 8, 1975
Page Two

"The duties of the park and recreation commission
shall be as follows:

(a) To act in an advisory capacity to the city council,
city manager and the director of parks and recreation
in all matters concerning recreation and parks; and to
assist in the coordination of all public agencies and
civil organizations to provide sound recreation and
parks planning and programming.

(b) Coordinate with the recreation and parks director
in formulating policies on recreation services for
approval by the city council.

(c) Recommend to the city council and Unified School
Board the best joint use of recreation facilities.

(d) Recommend to the city council the advisability of
acquiring and/or utilizing various parcels of land for
recreation and parks purposes.

(e) To advise with the director of parks and recreation
on development of recreation areas, facilities, programs
and recreation services.

(f) Recommend the adoption of standards on organization,
personnel, areas and facilities, program and financial
support of recreation and park activities.

(g) Make periodic inventories of recreation services
that exist or may be needed and interpret the needs of
the public to the city council and the director of parks
and recreation.

(h) Advise the director of parks and recreation in the
preparation of the annual budget request and on long
range recreation and parks capital improvement program.

(i) Consider all requests for permanent facilities to
be erected on park area.

(j) Promote and stimulate public interest in the parks
and recreation department through interpretation of the
policies and functions.

(k) Aid in coordinating the recreation services with
the programs of other governmental agencies and voluntary
organizations."

FACTUAL SITUATION NO. 2:

The chairman of the Vista Beautification Committee is a real
estate broker representing a client who owns real property which
the City proposes to purchase for a park site. The client has
promised a commission of over $250 upon successful consummation

Cont'd....

Fair Political Practices Commission
July 8, 1975
Page Three

of the sale, within 12 months before negotiating on behalf of the
client with the Director of Parks and Recreation and the City
Attorney. Successful negotiations will result in a recommendation
to the City Council for purchase, and the real estate broker plans
to urge City Council approval of such recommendation.

The Vista Beautification Committee is an advisory body of
the City created by resolution, a copy of which is attached hereto,
and composed of from seven to twenty appointees of the Mayor, confirmed by the City Council. Neither the chairman nor the members
are paid for their service. The powers and duties of the Committee
are set forth in the resolution, as follows:

"VISTA BEAUTIFICATION COMMITTEE
OBJECTIVES AND RESPONSIBILITIES

I. To recommend to the City Council, through the
City Manager and affected city departments, overall
plans and policies for a long-range program for making
Vista a more attractive and prospective city and more pleasant place
in which to live, including, but not limited to:

A. A general policy and effectuating regu-
lations covering all tree and shrub planting as
well as other means of beautification to enhance:

1. The approaches to the city.

2. The downtown area.

3. New subdivisions.

4. Retention or replacement of existing
trees.

5. Care of existing trees and permission
for removal.

6. The elimination of nuisances, junk
heaps, dumps, eyesores, stagnant
pools and other objectionable areas
as defined in existing ordinances and
city regulations.

II. To recommend specific programs and projects
relating to beautification to be:

A. Planned, developed, and maintained by the
city government.

B. Planned, developed, and maintained by civic
organizations with the cooperation of the Chamber of
Commerce.

Cont'd....

Fair Political Practices Commission
July 8, 1975
Page Four


        C. Planned and developed by civic organiza-
tions and maintained by the city.
        III. To organize:

        A. City-wide clean-up and paint-up campaigns
to be participated in by Girl and Boy Scouts,
school children and civic organizations.

        B. Beautification projects by blocks or areas
to be participated in by residents or businessmen.

        IV. To develop a public attitude and spirit that
will initiate and support whatever city ordinances are
necessary to insure maintenance of property values
through high standards of cleanliness, attractive land-
scaping, building standards, or urban renewal, financed
and supported locally.

        V. To coordinate efforts of all local organizations
having an interest in beautification and civic improvement, and to develop and encourage such interests.

        The Motto of this Committee shall be:

        "CITY BEAUTIFICATION IS AN INVESTMENT - IN
PROPERTY VALUES - IN GOOD LIVING - AND IN
CHARACTER."

QUESTION NO. 1:

        Are either the attorney or the real estate broker in the
above factual situations "public officials" within the meaning
of Section 87100 of the Government Code?

QUESTION NO. 2:

        If the answer to Question No. 1 with regard to the attorney
is YES, is the attorney prohibited from attempting to influence
the decision of the Planning Commission and City Council on behalf
of his client if he states to the Planning Commission and the City
Council that he is appearing on behalf of the client as a private
attorney, and not as a member of the Parks and Recreation Commission?

QUESTION NO. 3:

        If the answer to Question No. 1 with regard to the real estate
broker is YES, is the real estate broker prohibited from attempting
to influence the recommendation of the Director of Parks and Recreation and the City Attorney and the decision of the City Council if
the real estate broker states that the broker is appearing on behalf
of the client as a private real estate broker and not as the chairman or a member of the Vista Beautification Committee?

                                                        Cont'd....

Fair Political Practices Commission
July 8, 1975
Page Five

It is our view that the above factual situations and
questions, or ones similar thereto, will arise in numerous
instances throughout the State since most cities and counties
have a large variety of advisory commissions and boards,
and many of the members of same are professionals who,
in the course of their business or practice, represent clients
in their relations with the city or county. As a consequence,
we believe the questions raised here are matters of state-wide
concern and of interest to thousands of individuals and hundreds
of public entities and their legal advisers.

Thank you for your consideration in this regard.

Very truly yours,

[signature]

PAUL B. PRESSMAN
City Attorney

PBP/jd

att.

RESOLUTION NO. 64

RESOLUTION OF THE CITY COUNCIL OF THE CITY OF VISTA
AUTHORIZING AND DIRECTING THE CREATION OF A COMMITTEE
TO BE KNOWN AND DESIGNATED AS THE VISTA BEAUTIFICATION
COMMITTEE

WHEREAS, the City Council of the City of Vista does it
desirable and necessary that the City of Vista embark upon and
prepare a plan or system for the beautification of the City of
Vista, and

WHEREAS, many residents of the City of Vista have expressed an interest in serving the City of Vista in an advisory
capacity with a view to implementing and creating and carrying
into effect an orderly system and plan for the beautification of
the City of Vista, and

WHEREAS, the City Council of the City of Vista being
fully advised in the premises.

NOW, THEREFORE, BE IT RESOLVED BY THE CITY COUNCIL OF
THE CITY OF VISTA AS FOLLOWS:

Section 1: That there is hereby created an advisory
committee to be known and designated as the Vista Beautification
Committee.

Section 2: Said committee shall consist of representative members of the community. The members of said committee shall,
so far as possible, represent interested groups advocating the
beautification of the City of Vista such as service clubs, women's
organizations, and garden clubs.

Section 3: The Mayor of the City of Vista is hereby
authorized and directed to appoint interested persons to said committee, subject to the approval and consent of the City Council.

Section 4: Said Vista Beautification Committee shall
act in an advisory capacity and shall submit reports and recommendations to the Parks and Recreation Commission of the City of
Vista and shall further submit reports and recommendations to the
City Council of the City of Vista.

-1-

Section 5: The Staff of the City of Vista, including
the City Manager and the Planning Director, are hereby authorized
and directed to give the members of said committee whatever assist-
ance,may be necessary to accomplish the purposes of the Vista
Beautification Committee.

Section 6: The Vista Beautification Committee shall be
a standing committee and shall consist of not less than five (5)
nor more than fifteen (15) members.

PASSED AND ADOPTED this 10th day of August, 1965, by the
following roll call vote:

AYES: Councilmen: Goostree, Doyle, Cunningham, Fotheringham,
and Gillette

NOES: Councilmen: None

ABSENT: Councilmen: None


J.H. FOTHERINGHAM, MAYOR


ATTEST:

LETHA D. FINCH, CITY CLERK

sections.question:
NO. 1:
Are either the attorney or the real estate broker in the above factual situations "public officials" within the meaning of Section 87100 of the Government Code?

QUESTION NO. 2:
If the answer to Question No. 1 with regard to the attorney is YES, is the attorney prohibited from attempting to influence the decision of the Planning Commission and City Council on behalf of his client if he states to the Planning Commission and the City Council that he is appearing on behalf of the client as a private attorney, and not as a member of the Parks and Recreation Commission?

QUESTION NO. 3:
If the answer to Question No. 1 with regard to the real estate broker is YES, is the real estate broker prohibited from attempting to influence the recommendation of the Director of Parks and Recreation and the City Attorney and the decision of the City Council if the real estate broker states that the broker is appearing on behalf of the client as a private real estate broker and not as the chairman or a member of the Vista Beautification Committee?

It is our view that the above factual situations and questions, or ones similar thereto, will arise in numerous instances throughout the State since most cities and counties have a large variety of advisory commissions, committees and boards, and many of the members of same are professionals who, in the course of their business or practice, represent clients in their relations with the city or county. As a consequence, we believe the questions raised here are matters of state-wide concern and of interest to thousands of individuals and hundreds of public entities and their legal advisers.

Thank you for your consideration in this regard.

Thank you for your consideration in this regard.
